prompt: Create a polished journal-paper neural network architecture diagram on a white background, landscape 16:9, clean vector-like boxes and arrows, black sans-serif labels. Title: AGCRN architecture for station dep/arr forecasting. Show every component in this exact flow: Input X [B, 12, N, F] -> learnable node embeddings E [N, d] -> adaptive adjacency A = softmax(ReLU(E E^T)) -> Chebyshev support set [I, A, ...] -> stacked AVWDCRNN encoder, 2 AGCRNCell layers, unrolled over t=1..12 -> last hidden state H_T [B, N, hidden] -> End Conv2d kernel 1 x hidden -> reshape and permute -> output Y_hat [B, 12, N, 2], channels dep and arr. Add a small inset named Adaptive Graph Conv: support powers, node-adaptive weights W(E), bias b(E), graph convolution output. Add a second small inset named Recurrent Cell: concat x_t and h_{t-1}, gates z and r, candidate h_c, new h_t. Use muted blue, green, amber, and gray accents. Keep labels short and readable. No logos, no watermark, no fake citations, no decorative background.

Create a detailed module diagram for AGCRNCell and AVWGCN, journal-paper style, white background, landscape 16:9. Use exact readable English labels and thin arrows. Left side: inputs x_t [B,N,F], h_{t-1} [B,N,H], node embeddings E [N,d]. Center top: Adaptive adjacency A = softmax(ReLU(E E^T)); support set S_0=I, S_1=A, S_k=2 A S_{k-1} - S_{k-2}. Center bottom: AVWGCN gate branch with concat[x_t, h_{t-1}] -> node-adaptive weights W_gate(E), bias b_gate(E) -> sigmoid -> split z and r. Lower branch: AVWGCN update with concat[x_t, z * h_{t-1}] -> W_update(E), b_update(E) -> tanh -> h_c. Right side: h_t = r * h_{t-1} + (1-r) * h_c. Add a small parameter pool block: weights_pool [d, K, C_in, C_out], bias_pool [d, C_out]. Color-code graph construction, gate branch, update branch, and output state. Avoid extra modules not listed.

Create a polished journal-paper neural network architecture diagram on a white background, landscape 16:9, clean vector-like boxes and arrows, black sans-serif labels. Title: Graph WaveNet time + net-flow objective. Show every component in this exact flow: Historical input X [B,12,883,38] -> permute to [B,F,N,T] -> Start Conv 1x1, 38 to 32 residual channels -> fused graph support A_fused -> 2 blocks x 3 dilated temporal layers with dilations 1,2,4 -> sum skip connections -> ReLU -> End Conv 1x1, 128 to 256 -> last temporal hidden state -> time-conditioned horizon readout. Show the fused support branch above: node embeddings E -> adaptive support A_adp, OD forward top-k20, OD reverse top-k20, learnable softmax relation weights init 0.95,0.025,0.025 -> A_fused. Show the future-time branch below: future time features [B,12,7] with hour, day-of-week, month, weekend -> MLP 7 to 16 to 16 -> per-horizon time embedding. Show readout: concatenate hidden + time embedding -> Conv 1x1 272 to 256 -> Conv 1x1 256 to 2 -> output Y_hat [B,12,883,2] dep and arr. Add training objective box: L = MAE_dep_arr in log1p space + 0.10 * normalized MAE_net in count space, net=arr-dep. Use muted blue for temporal path, green for graph support, amber for future-time branch, red accent for auxiliary loss. No logos, no watermark, no fake citations.

Create a detailed module diagram for one Graph WaveNet dilated temporal block and the fused graph support, journal-paper style, white background, landscape 16:9. Use exact readable English labels. Left input: residual stream x [B,32,N,T]. Temporal gate section: left-padded causal Conv2d filter, dilation d, kernel 1x2 -> tanh; parallel left-padded causal Conv2d gate, dilation d, kernel 1x2 -> sigmoid; multiply to produce gated activation. Graph convolution section: support powers [I, A_fused, A_fused^2] -> node convolution -> concatenate -> 1x1 MLP -> dropout. Residual section: add original residual x -> BatchNorm -> updated residual output. Skip section: gated activation -> 1x1 skip Conv -> skip contribution, summed across all blocks. Above the block, show fused support formula: A_adp = softmax(ReLU(E E^T)); relation stack = A_adp, OD_forward, OD_reverse; relation weights = softmax(logits); A_fused = weighted sum. Show repeating schedule: block 1 dilations 1,2,4; block 2 dilations 1,2,4. Keep labels readable, use arrows, avoid extra components not listed.

Create a polished journal-paper neural network architecture diagram on a white background, landscape 16:9, clean vector-like boxes and arrows, black sans-serif labels. Title: TFT-style quantile model for station forecasting. Show every component in this exact flow: Historical multi-source input X [B,12,N,88] -> reshape per station [B*N,12,F] -> Gated Feature Projection, tanh(value linear) * sigmoid(gate linear), dropout, LayerNorm -> LSTM encoder -> encoded history keys and values. Show future-known time branch: future time features [B,12,7] -> Linear + GELU + LayerNorm -> future queries. Show static branch: station id -> station embedding [N,16] -> static projection -> add to future queries. Show Multihead Temporal Attention, 4 heads: queries attend to encoded history -> attention context. Show fusion: concatenate query + attention context -> post-attention gate, Linear + GELU + Dropout + LayerNorm -> Quantile Head -> reshape to [B,12,N,2,3]. Output labels: dep q10 q50 q90; arr q10 q50 q90. Add loss box: Pinball Loss in log1p space. Add inverse transform and interval box: q50 point forecast, q10-q90 interval. Use muted blue, purple, green, amber, and gray accents. No logos, no watermark, no fake citations.

Create a detailed module diagram for the TFT-style attention and monotone quantile head, journal-paper style, white background, landscape 16:9. Use exact readable English labels and thin arrows. Left top: encoded history from LSTM, K,V [B*N, lag=12, hidden]. Left bottom: future time projection [B, horizon=12, hidden] plus station static context [N, hidden] -> decoder query Q [B*N, horizon, hidden]. Center: Multihead Temporal Attention, 4 heads, with attention matrix [horizon x lag] and output context C. Right center: concatenate [Q, C] -> Linear -> GELU -> Dropout -> LayerNorm, labeled post-attention gate. Right: Quantile Head linear hidden to output_dim * 3, reshape to dep and arr. Lower right: monotone construction q10 = raw_0, q50 = q10 + softplus(raw_1), q90 = q50 + softplus(raw_2), prevents quantile crossing. Bottom row: Pinball Loss for quantiles 0.1, 0.5, 0.9; net-flow quantiles: q10 = arr_q10 - dep_q90, q50 = arr_q50 - dep_q50, q90 = arr_q90 - dep_q10. Include a small note: attention weights exported for lag interpretability. Do not include unrelated TFT variable selection networks.
